content.verdict: Kempinski Ishtar is the Dead Sea's grande dame, a series of terraced gardens and seven infinity pools cascading toward the lowest beach on earth. Honeymooners float in the hyper-saline water at sunset with views across to the lights of Jerusalem and Bethlehem — an unforgettable geographic backdrop.
content.best_room: The Panoramic Suite Ishtar faces the Dead Sea with a private plunge pool and outdoor daybed — unbeatable at dusk when the Judean hills glow pink. For couples wanting the absolute top, the villa-style Issis suites come with butler service and a private garden leading to the beach.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Descent to the lowest place on earth
itinerary_7_nights.description: Arrive via the 430-metre descent from Amman. Settle into your suite, stroll the cascade of pools to the private beach and watch the sunset float with complimentary champagne as the sea turns pink.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Ishtar Spa ritual day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Spend the morning in the 9,000 square metre Anantara-run Ishtar Spa with couples mud-wrap, hammam scrub and a 90-minute massage. Long lunch at Luigi's Italian restaurant by the pools.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Baptism Site and Mount Nebo
itinerary_7_nights.description: Short drive to Bethany-Beyond-the-Jordan where Jesus was baptised, followed by Mount Nebo for Moses's view of the Holy Land and mosaic-rich Madaba for lunch before returning for a sunset float.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Wadi Mujib adventure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 20 minutes south to Mujib Biosphere Reserve for the Siq Trail — wading upstream through a canyon to a hidden waterfall. Back to the hotel for rest, drinks at Al Nafoura and dinner at Ishtar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Float day and beach picnic
itinerary_7_nights.description: A slow day of floating, mud application, freshwater pool swims and a beach-side picnic arranged by the kitchen. Evening reflexology treatment and late dinner on the terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Jerash day trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Two-hour drive north to Jerash's Roman colonnades and back via Amman for late lunch at Sufra before returning to the Dead Sea for a final stargazing session on your private pool deck.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell
itinerary_7_nights.description: Final sunrise float, late breakfast and a slow transfer up to Amman airport or onward south to Petra and Wadi Rum via the scenic King's Highway route.

caveats: The Dead Sea is receding about one metre per year, so the walk from the hotel's sea-level terrace down to the waterline is longer each season — request a beach buggy if mobility is a concern.
Summer temperatures can exceed 42C and the humidity around the sea is high, making June to August challenging for honeymoon outings outside the air-conditioned zones and shaded pools.
Dead Sea water is extremely salty and stings any small cut or shaved skin, so plan shaving and waxing for after the float and do not submerge your face or splash water near your eyes.

faqs.question: Is this hotel good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — the private beach, seven pools, Ishtar Spa and plunge-pool suites make it one of the Middle East's most romantic wellness resorts. Book a sea-facing suite and honeymoon package for the full effect.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: March to May and September to November offer comfortable 25-30C days at the Dead Sea. Winter is mild and perfect for floating; summer is punishingly hot but compensated by pools and strong air conditioning.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Reserve a sea-facing Panoramic Suite or Issis villa, a couples mud-wrap at Ishtar Spa, the Wadi Mujib Siq Trail through a guide and sunset champagne float service on the private beach.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, the resort welcomes families and has a strong kids club, so for maximum honeymoon calm book a suite with a private plunge pool and reserve the adults-preferred upper pools for your day-to-day.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: The Panoramic Suite Ishtar with private plunge pool is the honeymoon sweet spot, pairing full Dead Sea view with the privacy of your own terrace. Issis villa suites go a step further with a butler and garden.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly into Queen Alia International Airport (AMM). The Dead Sea is 65 kilometres west, a 55-minute drive down a steep road. The hotel arranges private transfers and Allenby Bridge crossings from Israel as well.
